mentions.note: Trading at 16x earnings (cheap) but CEO Jamie Dimon's cautious commentary historically crushes stock; wait for post-earnings reaction to lower entry.
Quarter likely amazing but watch CEO Dimon's cautious comments on call; tendency to talk down even strong results can crush stock. Wait to see if he's worried; pounce if stock drops end of day.
Reported weaker underwriting, CEO cautious on credit; stock hammered but bounces always follow; wait 1-2 days then gradually buy weakness.

Solid beat on 7% NII growth, 17% Marcus growth, 40% stock trading; but investment banking down 5% YoY and CEO Dimon's political risk warning spooked market. Stock down 5% in 2 days after 35% run—due for breather. Will be fine long-term.
Financials rallied on Trump's 'war won' peace declaration; moved up 1% as risk-off sentiment reversed to risk-on.
Barely up on Day 2; financial sector weakness despite hopes for hangover recovery from Iran-war downturn.
Terrific bank but many earnings inputs and cautious CEO often disappoint Wall Street; await quarterly results Tuesday.
Best bank in the world; balanced growth at 13x earnings (below market average); down 7% YTD on credit fears despite fortress balance sheet. Can buy and hold without expecting mega-tech returns.
Large banks showing real resilience despite year of underperformance; broad financial sector recovery hint ahead of employment data.
Good company but timing is wrong; wait until after SpaceX IPO pricing before buying to allow more informed decision.
Bank rally has staying power on moderately higher rates (NIM expansion), strong consumer (May retail +6.9% YoY), tame delinquencies; still cheap; 50%+ upside before reasonably priced.
